description: Upstream complement cascade nodes — every step proximal to C5a generation. C5aR1 antagonism is the *downstream* CP0 modality that comp-014 + §1.21 confirmed has zero direct natural-product coverage; comp-018 instead asks whether compounds at any of these UPSTREAM nodes can prevent C5a from being generated in the first place.
platform_thesis_anchor: complement-c5a-gout.md §2 — MSU crystals activate complement primarily via the classical pathway (IgM/CRP → C1q → C4b2a C3 convertase) and amplify via the alternative pathway (C3bBb). Therefore upstream C3 convertase inhibition is the most direct upstream-of-CP0 mechanism. Lectin pathway is non-dominant in MSU but ingestion-relevant (gut microbiota mannose patterns).

targets.msu_relevance: DOMINANT — C1q binds IgM-coated MSU crystals and CRP-coated MSU crystals (Wessig 2022 PMID 35296708). First step of the dominant MSU initiation pathway.
targets.intervention_direction: block C1q binding to MSU surface OR block C1r/C1s catalytic activation

targets.msu_relevance: DOMINANT — cleave C4 and C2 to form C4b2a, the classical-pathway C3 convertase
targets.intervention_direction: serine protease inhibition (overlap with C1-INH biology)

targets.msu_relevance: NON-DOMINANT for crystals themselves but possibly relevant for gut microbiota-derived mannose/glucan patterns that prime systemic complement tone
targets.intervention_direction: block MBL recognition OR MASP catalytic activity

targets.msu_relevance: narsoplimab-target; same logic as MBL upstream
DOMINANT — C4b2a generates surface C3b on MSU. The natural-product literature most consistently targets this complex (rosmarinic acid, tiliroside, biflavonoids).
targets.intervention_direction: destabilize convertase OR block C4b2a assembly OR mimic DAF/CD55 decay-acceleration

targets.msu_relevance: AMPLIFIER — C3bBb is the alternative-pathway feedback loop on MSU surfaces (deficient host-surface Factor H recruitment because MSU lacks sialic acid)
targets.intervention_direction: destabilize C3bBb OR upregulate Factor H
block Bb binding to C3b (iptacopan synthetic precedent)

targets.msu_relevance: AMPLIFIER — cleaves Factor B → Bb, rate-limiting in AP
PROTECTIVE — Factor H upregulation should DAMPEN MSU-surface AP amplification. Note: positive intervention (upregulate, not inhibit).
targets.intervention_direction: UPREGULATE expression OR enhance C3b-binding affinity

targets.msu_relevance: AMPLIFIER — stabilizes C3bBb on surfaces, extends half-life ~10-fold

ENGINEERING ANCHOR — comp-006/comp-012/H05 thread; soluble truncated DAF SCR1-4 is the OE engineering candidate. Natural-product upregulators of endogenous DAF would synergize with the engineered construct.
targets.intervention_direction: UPREGULATE host DAF expression (positive intervention) OR mimic DAF decay-acceleration via small molecule (rare class)

targets.msu_relevance: DOWNSTREAM of C5a but blocks sublytic MAC priming on host cells
TERMINAL UPSTREAM — last step before C5a is generated
the substrate whose cleavage produces C5a — included for completeness; eculizumab/zilucoplan/vilobelimab pharma anchors
INCLUDED FOR COMPLETENESS — comp-014 + §1.21 already empty; included so search expansion can confirm or refute
PROTECTIVE — natural inhibitor of C1r/C1s and MASPs; inhibits AP convertase per Jiang 2001 PMID 11733575
targets.intervention_direction: UPREGULATE host C1-INH OR exogenous C1-INH replacement

scope_notes: Per Brian framing 2026-05-08: scope is BROAD — fungal, plant, bacterial, marine, dietary, FDA-approved drug, classical TCM/Kampo/Ayurveda. Don't pre-narrow.

Sub-targets DAF/CD55, Factor H, C1-INH, CD59 are HOST regulators where the desired direction is UPREGULATION (positive intervention) — not inhibition. Do not penalize compounds that upregulate without inhibiting.
C5aR1 is included as a sanity check on the comp-014 / §1.21 finding (no direct natural-product C5aR1 antagonist). If comp-018 surfaces a hit here, that would be a novel finding.
